name: Tunnel Zone 3 — The Drowned Galleries Wandering Monsters
description: Wandering monster table for Zone 3 (Days 11–14): Seasonal flood zone, dwarven ruins, underground waterways. Wet, treacherous, and inhabited by things that prefer it that way. Check frequency: once per 8 hours of travel, once per 8 hours of rest.

results.text: 2d6 Giant Frogs (in flooded chambers, ambush from below) [PF2e Lvl 1 each]
1d4 Merrow (aquatic ogres; territorial in the deeper channels) [PF2e Lvl 4 each]
1 Chuul (in a submerged arch, reaches through) [PF2e Lvl 7]
2d6 Drowned Dwarf Zombies (from the old survey teams) [PF2e Lvl 1 each]
1 Sea Hag (living in a flooded dwarven vault, hoard of survey tools) [PF2e Lvl 4]
1d4 Kuo-Toa Monitors (D1/D2 preview; scouting this far forward) [PF2e Lvl 3 each]
1 Water Elemental, Medium (loose from a flooded binding circle in the ruins) [PF2e Lvl 5]
1d6 Draugr (ancient dwarven dead, awakened by the flooding) [PF2e Lvl 2 each]
1 Bog Mummy (preserved in mineral water for centuries) [PF2e Lvl 5]
1 Cave Troll + flooding — troll drives party into rising water (Athletics DC 18 to cross before it fills) [PF2e Lvl 5]
1 Aboleth (in a deep pool; does not pursue beyond water but casts from depth) [PF2e Lvl 12 — flee or negotiate]
1d4 Kuo-Toa Whips (further scouts; will tail the party and report to D1) [PF2e Lvl 4 each]
HAZARD — Partial collapse: DC 20 Reflex or trapped under rubble (DC 22 Athletics or 1 minute to free); blocks one corridor
1 Water Orm (ancient, vast, fills the main flooded channel) [PF2e Lvl 8 — circumvent, don't fight]
HAZARD — Flash flood: DC 22 Athletics to reach high ground; failure means 60 feet swept back, 4d6 bludgeoning
